_instructions.adding_a_post: 1) Add an entry to posts[] below. 2) Drop the .html file in /posts/. Done — card appears automatically.
_instructions.contact_form: Sign up free at formspree.io, create a form, paste the endpoint URL into formspreeEndpoint below.
_instructions.photo: Place photo.jpg (portrait, min 800x1000px) in the root folder alongside index.html.

meta.description: Senior AI Engineer specialising in Generative AI, RAG pipelines, LLM agents, and AI systems that work in production.

personal.name: Yashasvi Shukla
personal.availability: Open to remote collaborations · English & Hindi

headline: Building AI
That Works
In Production.
home.subheadline: Senior AI Engineer crafting RAG pipelines, LLM agents, and intelligent search systems for enterprise at scale.

bio: I'm a Senior AI Engineer at Zensar Technologies with 6+ years of experience building production-grade Generative AI systems — RAG pipelines, LLM agents, intelligent search, and everything in between.
I've led end-to-end AI delivery across aviation, telecom, and BFSI sectors, working with teams to turn complex retrieval and reasoning problems into reliable, scalable systems that actually ship.
I'm currently exploring the frontier of agentic AI — multi-agent orchestration, Google ADK, MCP and A2A protocols — and writing about what I learn along the way.

experience.description: Leading design and delivery of Generative AI solutions across aviation, telecom, and BFSI. End-to-end ownership from scoping to production deployment.
Led a team of 3 data scientists. Built GenAI POCs, customer support chatbots, and multi-stage intent classification systems.
NLP, sentiment analysis, incident routing automation, and ML models across healthcare and financial domains.

contact.intro: Building something interesting? Thinking through an AI system? Want to collaborate or just talk shop? I'm always up for a good conversation.
contact.availability: Currently open to remote collaborations and consulting. Response time: within 48 hours.

posts.title: Recursive Language Models: The Architecture of Infinite Thought
posts.excerpt: How AI broke the context barrier — from tree-shaped neural networks of the 2010s to the agent scaffolds processing ten million tokens today.

posts.title: Parent-Child Chunking: The RAG Pattern Nobody Talks About Enough
posts.excerpt: Standard chunking breaks context. Parent-child chunking preserves it — giving you retrieval precision and generation context at the same time.

posts.title: Inverted Index vs BM25: When You Need Custom Keyword Search
posts.excerpt: BM25 doesn't understand logical AND/OR. Here's how an inverted index with a logical query parser gives you search that actually behaves.

posts.title: Dense vs Sparse vs Hybrid: Choosing the Right Retrieval Strategy
posts.excerpt: Every RAG system faces this question. A practical breakdown of when each approach wins — and how to combine them without killing latency.

posts.title: LLM-as-a-Judge: Grading Your RAG Responses for Hallucinations
posts.excerpt: Using a language model to evaluate another sounds circular. Done right, it's one of the most practical tools for keeping production RAG honest.

posts.title: Query Expansion and Multi-Query: Getting More From Your Retriever
posts.excerpt: A single user query is rarely the best retrieval signal. Here's how query expansion, multi-query generation, and HyDE can dramatically improve recall.

posts.title: LLM Monitoring in Production: What I Learned the Hard Way
posts.excerpt: Deploying RAG without observability is flying blind. Here's what to track, which tools I've found useful, and what actually matters.
